D6 Display L5 Evaluate Reference

microLED: Every Pixel a Lamp

millions of inorganic LEDs, one per subpixel — perfect blacks like OLED, but far brighter and free of organic burn-in

microLED makes each subpixel a microscopic inorganic LED that emits its own light. Like OLED there is no backlight, so an off pixel is truly black; unlike OLED the emitter is inorganic, reaching far higher peak brightness with no burn-in. The catch is manufacturing — placing millions of perfect emitters by "mass transfer".

Drive the peak brightness and compare a self-emissive grid against an LCD whose backlight leaks through "black" pixels. On microLED the dark areas stay perfectly black at any brightness; on the LCD the floor rises with the backlight.
